{{Infobox group
| name = Travelers
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Travelers Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Travelers are individuals linked to specific [[Territories]], which serve as the source of their unique abilities. To become a [[Traveler]], an individual must undergo compatibility testing to ensure they can connect with a [[Territory]]. These beings are often characterized by their specialized powers, which vary significantly depending on their origin; for example, Travelers from [[Helgard]], [[Ornheim]], or [[Naraka]] can summon elemental blasts, weapons, or creatures, while those from [[Lirial]] focus on gathering and preserving information. Some Travelers, such as those from [[Endross]], are described as predatory scavengers, while others, like the [[Tartarus]] variety, function as soldiers. [[Valinhall Travelers]] are distinct in that their [[Territory]] was created specifically for the purpose of combat against other Travelers. Travelers are often identified by their specific attire, such as the [[steel]] armor of [[Tartarus]] or the feathered garments of [[Avernus]]. While many Travelers possess diverse and sometimes terrifying powers, they are often described as being psychologically unstable due to the stress of interacting with alien worlds. Their social roles vary from scouts and sentinels to guards or even political exiles serving temporary duties. In certain contexts, Travelers may be part of organized groups or tribal affiliations rather than following national loyalties. Additionally, if a [[Traveler]] loses control, they may become an [[Incarnation]].}}

== House of Blades ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Territories]] are for [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The white-haired man has killed [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5">''House of Blades'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Human guards ran when they realized [[Travelers]] were fighting.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Travelers are a common presence within the [[Damascan royal army]] camp. During their time in [[Avernus]], these individuals served as escorts for [[Alin]], though they faced numerous dangers including fireballs and giant birds. Despite their involvement, the actions of [[Adessa]] resulted in the loss of six Travelers' lives. Ultimately, there are concerns regarding whether the Travelers possess any actual utility against [[Kai]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Adessa]]'s actions wasted the lives of six [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Travelers]] are a common sight in the [[Damascan royal army]] camp.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Travelers]] are potentially useless against [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Travelers]] in [[Avernus]] escorted [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Other [[Travelers]] contended with various threats like giant birds and fireballs.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|During combat within the camp, Travelers demonstrated their ability to summon objects from their respective [[Territories]]. One such [[Territory]] is [[Valinhall]], which exists solely for the purpose of killing other Travelers. While some individuals within this group actively avoid [[Grandmasters]], others possess the capability to attack [[Incarnations]], though they remain susceptible to being deceived or waylaid by them. Looking toward the future, [[Leah]] intends to dispatch parties of Travelers on upcoming missions while positioning herself behind a defensive wall composed of golems and her own kind.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Travelers]] engaged in combat within the camp.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c1">''City of Light'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Travelers]] can call things from their [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]] that exists only to kill other [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|There are [[Travelers]] who avoid [[Grandmasters]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Travelers]] can attack [[Incarnations]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Travelers]] can be waylaid or deceived by [[Incarnations]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] plans to send parties of [[Travelers]] out later.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is standing behind a wall of golems and [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate groups]]
